name: openai-latest-research

must_answer: What are the key architectures OpenAI has introduced or improved?
What training methodologies, datasets, or techniques produced recent advancements?
How do their latest models compare technically to prior versions?
What novel capabilities do new models demonstrate?
What are the main limitations or areas needing further research for their models?
How have new model releases influenced adoption or applications across domains?
What are the major technical challenges they've overcome recently?
What technical ethics or safety measures have they implemented?
sub_questions: What new architectural innovations has OpenAI developed since its last major model release?
What datasets or training methodologies drove recent improvements?
How do recent models compare technically to previous versions?
What novel capabilities does the latest generation of models provide?
What limitations or areas needing further research do the latest OpenAI models exhibit?
How has the latest research influenced practical applications?
What are the main technical challenges OpenAI has recently overcome?
What technical ethics or safety approaches have been implemented in recent releases?
brief.evaluation_rubric: Sources must be credible primary or reputed secondary. Must cover recent technical progress, not just product features. Must avoid commercial aspects. Comprehensive coverage of technical merits, comparisons, innovations, applications, and limitations expected from multiple reputable sources.

sources.title: OpenAI
sources.snippet:
Title: An OpenAI model has disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry
URL: https://openai.com/index/model-disproves-discrete-geometry-conjecture
Date: 2026-05-20T00:00:00.000Z
An OpenAI model solved the 80-year-old unit distance problem, disproving a major conjecture in discrete geometry and marking a milestone in AI-driven mathematics.

---

Title: New commission to provide insight as OpenAI builds the world’s best-equipped nonprofit
URL: https://openai.com/index/nonprofit-commission-guidance
Date: 2025-04-02T12:00:00.000Z
Already a nonprofit, and already using AI to help people solve hard problems, OpenAI aims to build the best-equipped nonprofit the world has ever seen—combining potentially historic financial resources with something even more powerful: technology that can scale human ingenuity itself.

---

Title: Advancing voice intelligence with new models in the API
URL: https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api
Date: 2026-05-07T10:00:00.000Z
Explore new realtime voice models in the OpenAI API that can reason, translate, and transcribe speech, enabling more natural and intelligent voice experiences.

sources.title: TechCrunch
sources.snippet:
Title: Elon Musk has given up on solar power (on Earth)
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/23/elon-musk-has-given-up-on-solar-power-on-earth/
Date: 2026-05-23T13:00:00.000Z
Elon Muks's xAI has gone all in on natural gas, while SpaceX is obsessed with orbital data centers. What happened to the "solar-electric economy" he promised?

---

Title: Blue Origin cleared to fly New Glenn mega-rocket after April mishap
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/22/blue-origin-cleared-to-fly-new-glenn-mega-rocket-after-april-mishap/
Date: 2026-05-22T21:37:17.000Z
Jeff Bezos' rocket company confirmed an engine failure led to the loss of an AST SpaceMobile satellite last month, but offered little detail.

---

Title: Meta quietly launches a new Reddit-like app called Forum
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/22/meta-quietly-launches-a-new-reddit-like-app-called-forum/
Date: 2026-05-22T14:24:16.000Z
The company describes the app as a "dedicated space built for deeper discussions, real answers and communities you care about."

sources.title: Microsoft Research
sources.snippet:
Title: GridSFM: A new, small foundation model for the electric grid
URL: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/gridsfm-a-new-small-foundation-model-for-the-electric-grid/
Date: 2026-05-13T16:00:20.000Z
Introducing GridSFM, a small foundation model that can predict AC optimal power flow in milliseconds, boosting efficiency and unlocking cost savings. Learn how GridSFM gives grid operators direct visibility into congestion, stability, and system health. The post GridSFM: A new, small foundation model for the electric grid appeared first on Microsoft Research.

---

Title: Advancing AI for materials with MatterSim: experimental synthesis, faster simulation, and multi-task models
URL: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/advancing-ai-for-materials-with-mattersim-experimental-synthesis-faster-simulation-and-multi-task-models/
Date: 2026-05-12T13:00:00.000Z
MatterSim is expanding what AI can do for materials science—from faster large-scale simulations to MatterSim-MT, a new multi-task model for simulating properties beyond potential energy surfaces alone. The post Advancing AI for materials with MatterSim: experimental synthesis, faster simulation, and multi-task models appeared first on Microsoft Research.

---

Title: MagenticLite, MagenticBrain, Fara1.5: An agentic experience optimized for small models
URL: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/magenticlite-magenticbrain-fara1-5-an-agentic-experience-optimized-for-small-models/
Date: 2026-05-21T17:00:00.000Z
MagenticLite is an agentic system for small models that works across the browser and local file system in a single workflow. It combines specialized models and orchestration to support efficient agentic performance on everyday tasks. The post MagenticLite, MagenticBrain, Fara1.5: An agentic experience optimized for small models appeared first on Microsoft Research.

Title: How a Gemma model helped discover a new potential cancer therapy pathway
URL: https://deepmind.google/blog/how-a-gemma-model-helped-discover-a-new-potential-cancer-therapy-pathway/
Date: 2025-10-23T18:22:55.000Z
We’re launching a new 27 billion parameter foundation model for single-cell analysis built on the Gemma family of open models.

---

Title: Uniting biological toolkits for a new approach to ALS
URL: https://deepmind.google/blog/uniting-biological-toolkits-for-a-new-approach-to-als/
Date: 2026-05-16T07:53:11.000Z
Co-Scientist unites Boston Children’s Hospital and MIT’s labs to explore new RNA-based treatments for ALS.

---

Title: T5Gemma: A new collection of encoder-decoder Gemma models
URL: https://deepmind.google/blog/t5gemma-a-new-collection-of-encoder-decoder-gemma-models/
Date: 2025-10-25T18:14:00.000Z

{"content":{"summary":{"value":"This paper provides a pioneering study on scaled evaluation of training data memorization issues in aligned Large Language Models (LLMs). The paper effectively defines memorization as the generation of at least 50 tokens that match training data. The authors created AUXDATASET, a 10-terabyte dataset merging four of the largest published language model training datasets, enabling systematic evaluation of the lower bound of training data memorization.
